maxContractTerm: No statutory maximum found. Louisiana law neither mandates nor caps the term of a contract between a public school board (city, parish, or other local public school board — the Louisiana terms for school districts) and a private student-transportation / school-bus contractor. The controlling enabling provision is La. R.S. 17:158(A)(4): "For the purposes of this Subsection, any city, parish, or other local public school board may employ school bus operators as defined in R.S. 17:491. However, nothing in this Section shall prohibit a city, parish, or other local public school board from entering into contracts or mutual agreements for providing school bus transportation." (https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=79904, re-verified 2026-06-23.) Neither R.S. 17:158 (subsections A-K, none of which cap term, mandate bidding, or set renewal mechanics for private contracts) nor any other Title 17 transportation section (R.S. 17:158.1-158.7, 17:160-166, 17:491-500) prescribes a maximum award term for a private operator. BESE's implementing regulation, LAC 28:CXIII (Bulletin 119 — Louisiana School Transportation Specifications and Procedures, February 2023 publication), addresses fleet/group insurance, vehicle specifications, driver qualifications, route assignment, and compensation, but does not set a maximum contract term for private contractors. The general state procurement framework that might otherwise impose a duration cap (the Public Bid Law, R.S. 38:2211 et seq.) does not reach service contracts: Louisiana Legislative Auditor, Public Bid Law FAQ (Revised 02/2026), A.88 ("No. The Public Bid Law is relevant to the procurement of materials and supplies and public works, not services") (https://app.lla.state.la.us/llala.nsf/87BD5C74CB17E03686257AB8006F37DE/$FILE/Public%20Bid%20Law%20FAQ.pdf). A practical structural ceiling exists for the dominant Louisiana model — the individually contracted owner-operator — through La. R.S. 17:497 / LAC 28:CXIII §1703 "frozen mileage" guarantees of 7 years (new bus) or 5 years (used bus not more than 5 model-years old), but those are compensation-protection floors for an individual owner-operator, not statutory caps on a fleet/route contract.
rebidRequired: Not required by state law. Louisiana imposes no statutory or regulatory duty on a city, parish, or other local public school board to conduct a competitive procurement (sealed bid, RFP, RFQ, or competitive negotiation) at the expiration of a private student-transportation contract. Three converging authorities establish this: (i) La. R.S. 17:158(A)(4) authorizes "contracts or mutual agreements for providing school bus transportation" without prescribing any selection method or rebid trigger (https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=79904); (ii) Louisiana's Public Bid Law, R.S. 38:2211 et seq., is a public-works and materials-and-supplies statute and does not reach service contracts, including transportation services — confirmed by the Louisiana Legislative Auditor's Public Bid Law FAQ (Revised 02/2026), A.87 ("No. Local governments and political subdivisions are not required to advertise, receive bids or engage in competitive negotiations for contracts for professional services") and A.88 ("No. The Public Bid Law is relevant to the procurement of materials and supplies and public works, not services") (https://app.lla.state.la.us/llala.nsf/87BD5C74CB17E03686257AB8006F37DE/$FILE/Public%20Bid%20Law%20FAQ.pdf); (iii) the Louisiana Department of Education's Office of School Choice FAQ Public Bid Law (rev. 07/2025) treats only public-works contracts as subject to mandatory competitive bidding for school boards (https://doe.louisiana.gov/docs/default-source/school-choice/faq-public-bid-law.pdf). LASBO Workshop 11 (Public Bid Law) materials state expressly: "Public Bid Law does not apply to the purchase of Services — Professional or Otherwise" (https://cdn.ymaws.com/lasbo.org/resource/collection/E9C17B05-DDC1-4796-A03E-2665F6A408BE/Workshop_11_Public_Bid_Law.pdf). The Louisiana Procurement Code (R.S. 39:1551 et seq.) governs executive-branch state agencies, not local school boards, unless a local board affirmatively adopts it under R.S. 38:2212.1; per LLA FAQ, that option pertains to materials/supplies, not services. Caveat for the owner-operator system: when a school bus route operated by an individual school-bus operator (LEA-owned bus or contracted owner-operator) becomes vacant, R.S. 17:493.1 dictates a tenure-and-seniority-based offer sequence (greatest-seniority tenured operator first, then in order of seniority, then probationary, then substitute, and where the retiring operator owns the bus, a buyer of the bus at appraised value) — this is the Louisiana mechanism for filling a route at vacancy and is decidedly not a competitive rebid.
renewalMechanics: No statewide renewal mechanism is prescribed; renewal terms are a matter of contract and local board policy. Because Louisiana law neither caps the initial term nor compels a rebid (see above), the statutes and BESE rules simply do not regulate renewal of a transportation services contract — they neither authorize nor restrict one-year extensions, CPI/index adjustments, fuel escalators, automatic rollover/evergreen renewal, mutual written agreement, agency approval, or board approval as a renewal mechanism. The only state-imposed contract-continuation features sit on the school-bus-operator side, not the contractor-services side: (a) tenure for individual school-bus operators hired before July 1, 2012, under R.S. 17:492 (operators hired on or after July 1, 2012 are not tenure-eligible and may be removed per local personnel policy — LAC 28:CXIII §309(B)); (b) the "frozen mileage" protection in R.S. 17:497 and LAC 28:CXIII §1703(B), which guarantees that a contracted owner-operator's compensated mileage cannot be penalized by reduction "for a period of seven years when a new bus is purchased or five years when a used bus not more than five model year old is purchased" (verbatim from LAC 28:CXIII §1703(B)); (c) the operator-protection floor in R.S. 17:497.1 — CORRECTED FROM STAGE 1: this provision applies in St. Landry Parish only (statute states "the provisions of this section shall apply in St. Landry Parish only"); it is not a statewide compensation protection. General appropriations/anti-deficiency principles applicable to all Louisiana local government contracts apply, but no specific renewal-procedure statute applies to a school transportation services contract. Charter schools that provide their own transportation must submit a Transportation Plan to LDOE annually by July 31 (BESE Authorized Charter School Transportation Plan; https://doe.louisiana.gov/docs/default-source/school-choice/charter-transportation-plan.pdf), but this is a charter-school plan-submission requirement, not a private-contract renewal rule.
statutoryCitation: Primary statutory authority: La. R.S. 17:158(A)(4) — "For the purposes of this Subsection, any city, parish, or other local public school board may employ school bus operators as defined in R.S. 17:491. However, nothing in this Section shall prohibit a city, parish, or other local public school board from entering into contracts or mutual agreements for providing school bus transportation." Official text: https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=79904 (Louisiana State Legislature; amendment history through Acts 2023, No. 362, §1; re-verified 2026-06-23). Companion school-bus-operator statutes: La. R.S. 17:491 (definition of "school bus operator" — "any employee of any city, parish, or other local public school board whose duty it is to transport students in any board school bus", https://legis.la.gov/Legis/Law.aspx?d=81109); R.S. 17:492 (probation/tenure of bus operators — tenure abolished prospectively for operators hired on or after July 1, 2012, per Acts 2012, No. 704); R.S. 17:493 (removal procedures); R.S. 17:493.1 (filling route vacancies — tenure-and-seniority sequence, plus owner-bus succession at appraised value for a retiring operator whose bus is within 5 model years and who is retiring for documented physical disability); R.S. 17:496 (minimum salaries — https://legis.la.gov/Legis/Law.aspx?d=81117); R.S. 17:497 (compensation/operational schedules / frozen-mileage authority); R.S. 17:497.1 (operator compensation protection after court-ordered route changes — APPLIES IN ST. LANDRY PARISH ONLY, Acts 1970, No. 333, https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=81120); R.S. 17:158.2 (LEA purchase and resale of buses to operators); R.S. 17:158.7 (lease of buses from school bus operators). Primary regulatory authority: LAC 28:CXIII (Bulletin 119 — Louisiana School Transportation Specifications and Procedures), promulgated by BESE pursuant to R.S. 17:158, 17:160-161, 17:164-166. Key provisions include §307 (Retaining School Bus Drivers); §309 (Tenure and Termination); §1505 (Routes: Filling Vacancies — cross-referencing R.S. 17:493.1); §1701-1703 (Compensation; Frozen Mileage); §2101-2107 (Transporting Students with Disabilities); §2503-2509 (Purchase/Sale/Lease of School Buses); §2513 (Insurance for School Buses, incl. (D) — "Insurance for Contracted Services. State law authorizes LEAs contracting for the use of privately owned school buses to procure contracts on a fleet or group basis for the owners who are insuring the vehicles."). Official Bulletin 119 PDF (February 2023 publication; historical notes show amendments through LR 49:244 (February 2023) and LR 45:35 (January 2019)): the doa.la.gov primary URL https://www.doa.la.gov/media/b0ehowxe/28v113.pdf returned 404 on re-verification 2026-06-23 — the equivalent text was verified via mirror at https://ebrschools.org/CAPS/Bulletins/Bulletin119.pdf (East Baton Rouge Parish CAPS) and the Cornell LII publication at https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/louisiana/title-28/part-CXIII. General procurement authority (NOT applicable to services): La. R.S. 38:2211 et seq. (Public Bid Law — public works and materials/supplies only; LLA Public Bid Law FAQ Rev. 02/2026, A.87-88: services contracts are outside the Public Bid Law for local political subdivisions).
districtVariance: Unknown. Substantial unofficial evidence suggests material variance in how Louisiana local school boards actually contract for student transportation — some parishes (e.g., Jefferson Parish, where First Student was awarded a five-year contract in 2016 per industry trade press) have used multi-year RFP-procured contracts with national operators while most Louisiana parishes retain the historical individual owner-operator / tenured-driver structure under R.S. 17:491-497 and Bulletin 119, with contracts at the individual bus-operator level. No state-level guidance document was found that uniformly interprets or directs how parishes must (or may) procure or renew a transportation services contract: no LDOE bulletin, no statewide BESE policy, no Legislative Auditor advisory, and no Attorney General opinion located that addresses (or harmonizes) term/rebid/renewal practices applied across districts to private transportation contractors. The LDOE Office of School Choice FAQ Public Bid Law and the LASBO Workshop 11 materials uniformly state that services contracts (including transportation) are outside the Public Bid Law, implying parishes have wide latitude on contract structure; whether that latitude is exercised uniformly across the 64+ parish/city school systems is not documented at the state level. Negative search path: searched legis.la.gov, doe.louisiana.gov, bese.louisiana.gov, app.lla.state.la.us (Legislative Auditor), the Louisiana AG opinions index (via web search), LASBO publications, and individual parish policy manuals (West Baton Rouge, Bossier, NOLA Public Schools, St. John, Zachary, Central, St. Mary, St. Tammany, Calcasieu, Orleans) — district policies recite R.S. 17:158 and adopt the "contract bus operator shall personally operate" rule, but do not adopt or describe a uniform term-length or rebid practice. Because evidence of uniform statewide application is absent and evidence of cross-parish variation is from industry press rather than statutory or agency sources, the most accurate label is Unknown.
specialEducation: Different service obligations, same contract framework. Louisiana does NOT establish a separate procurement statute, term-cap, rebid rule, or renewal mechanism for special-education / IEP / McKinney-Vento / foster-care / alternative transportation. The substantive service standards differ — LEAs must comply with the IDEA, Section 504, and BESE Bulletin 1706 (Regulations for Implementation of the Children with Exceptionalities Act) and must provide transportation as a related service when the IEP team so determines (LAC 28:CXIII §2101-2107) — but the legal authority to contract for those services remains La. R.S. 17:158(A)(4) and the same Public Bid Law / Procurement Code analysis (services are outside the Public Bid Law). Bulletin 119 Chapter 21 (Transporting Students with Disabilities) addresses service quality (curb-to-curb vs. door-to-door; bus attendants; ride time; extended-year program transportation under §2105; transportation by other-than-school-bus vehicles under §2107) but does not impose a separate contract-term, rebid, or renewal regime. Chapter 19 §1905 (Transportation of Students in Foster Care) similarly addresses service obligations (consistent with the federal ESSA mandate) without altering contract procurement. Bottom line: federal law (IDEA, Section 504, McKinney-Vento Homeless Education Assistance Improvements Act, ESSA Title IX foster-care provisions) drives the duty to transport and the service specifications; Louisiana state law does not layer a special-education-specific contract/procurement regime on top of the general R.S. 17:158(A)(4) authorization.
notesAndSpecialProvisions:
- Owner-operator system as the historical default. Louisiana's distinctive feature is that "school bus operator" is statutorily defined as "any employee of any city, parish, or other local public school board whose duty it is to transport students in any board school bus" (R.S. 17:491), even when the operator owns the bus. The dominant historical model is an individual driver who personally operates and (often) owns the bus, with tenure protection for pre-July 1, 2012 hires (R.S. 17:492; LAC 28:CXIII §309). Personal-operation requirement recurs across district policy manuals citing Bulletin 119.

- Tenure abolished prospectively. La. R.S. 17:492 (and LAC 28:CXIII §309(B)): "School bus operators starting employment with a school system on July 1, 2012, or thereafter are not eligible for tenure and may be removed from their position as provided by the personnel policy of the employing school board."

- Filling a vacant route — statutory sequence (R.S. 17:493.1; LAC 28:CXIII §1505): (1) most-senior tenured operator offered first, in seniority order; (2) full-time probationary operator; (3) substitute operator from approved list; (4) special owner-retirement provision allowing a buyer of the retiring operator's bus (at appraised value) to receive the route, where the bus is no more than 5 model-years old and the retirement is for documented physical disability. This is the closest Louisiana analogue to a "rebid at term-end" and is non-competitive.

- Frozen-mileage protection (R.S. 17:497; LAC 28:CXIII §1703(B)): "Frozen mileage guarantees that the contract owner/operator cannot be penalized by a reduction of compensated mileage … for a period of seven years when a new bus is purchased or five years when a used bus not more than five model year old is purchased." Operational structural effect: a parish that contracts with individual owner-operators effectively commits to a multi-year compensation floor.

- Court-ordered route change protection (R.S. 17:497.1) — CORRECTED: applies in St. Landry Parish only. The statute states: "the provisions of this section shall apply in St. Landry Parish only." It is NOT a statewide protection (Stage 1 wording corrected here).

- Services contracts outside the Public Bid Law. LLA Public Bid Law FAQ (Revised 02/2026), A.87-88: services — including transportation — are not subject to the Public Bid Law for local political subdivisions; competitive procurement is therefore discretionary, not mandatory. LDOE Office of School Choice FAQ confirms only public-works contracts trigger mandatory bidding for school boards. LASBO Workshop 11 states verbatim: "Public Bid Law does not apply to the purchase of Services — Professional or Otherwise."

- Materials/supplies thresholds (background, not a transportation-contract rule). For materials and supplies, parishes must competitively bid contracts at or over $60,000 effective February 1, 2026 (LLA FAQ A.1 / R.S. 38:2212(C)(1)); written/electronic quotes apply between $30,000 and $60,000. These thresholds reach a bus purchase but not a transportation services contract.

- Bus-purchase pooling and sale-to-operator authority. R.S. 17:158.2 and 17:158.3 authorize LEAs to purchase buses and resell to operators, and to pool bus purchases. R.S. 17:158.7 and Bulletin 119 §2507 authorize leases between LEAs and operators or authorized businesses.

- Fleet/group insurance contracts. LAC 28:CXIII §2513(D), verbatim: "Insurance for Contracted Services. State law authorizes LEAs contracting for the use of privately owned school buses to procure contracts on a fleet or group basis for the owners who are insuring the vehicles." This is an insurance-procurement authorization, not a competitive-procurement requirement for the underlying transportation services.

- Charter schools. La. R.S. 17:3996 makes charters exempt from most public-school statutes but lists exceptions. BESE-authorized charters that provide transportation must submit a Transportation Plan to LDOE annually by July 31 and abide by R.S. 17:158 and the approved plan (BESE Authorized Charter School Transportation Plan). Note: the LDOE charter plan document references R.S. 17:158 generally; older references to "R.S. 17:158(J)" in some materials reflect prior subsection lettering that has shifted following Acts 2023, No. 362 — current 17:158(J) addresses loading/unloading procedures.

- Cooperative purchasing / "piggyback." Available under the Public Bid Law (R.S. 38:2212.1) and the Louisiana Procurement Code (R.S. 39:1701 et seq.) for materials and supplies that bear on transportation (e.g., bus purchases) — but not the vehicle of choice for services contracts, which are unregulated for competitive procurement at the local-board level.

- Sole-source / emergency / small-purchase exemptions. LLA Public Bid Law FAQ details emergency, extreme-emergency, and other Public Bid Law exceptions for materials/supplies and public works; because transportation services are already outside the Public Bid Law, these exceptions are not directly relevant to a transportation-services contract.

- State approval. No general BESE/LDOE pre-approval required to enter a private transportation contract; LDOE School Bus Transportation Program provides training and technical assistance to transportation supervisors and private contractors, but this is technical-assistance, not pre-clearance.

- Discontinuation of service. R.S. 17:158(H) requires State Board approval before a parish eliminates transportation services. This is a service-discontinuation control, not a contract-renewal control.

- Recent legislative activity. R.S. 17:158 was most recently amended by Acts 2023, No. 362, §1, per the official Louisiana Legislature page (re-verified 2026-06-23). 2024 SB 416 was identified but did not result in a substantive amendment affecting contract term/rebid/renewal rules for private transportation contracts. The statute as currently published has subsections A through K; none cap term, mandate bidding, or set renewal mechanics for private contracts.
exceptionsUnresolved: All five fields were resolved on primary authority for the question presented — Louisiana's statutes and BESE regulations are EXPLICITLY SILENT on (and the Public Bid Law and Procurement Code by their own terms do not reach) max contract term, mandatory rebid, and renewal mechanics for private student-transportation services contracts. The District Variance field is the only one carrying a residual "Unknown" label, and that residual reflects the absence of state-level data on uniformity rather than an open legal question. One Stage 1 claim was REFUTED-AND-CORRECTED during adversarial validation: the operator-protection provision in R.S. 17:497.1 was characterized as a statewide protection in Stage 1; the official statutory text (https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=81120) expressly states "the provisions of this section shall apply in St. Landry Parish only." The Renewal Mechanics field and Notes were corrected accordingly. Additional minor correction: the Stage 1 reference to "R.S. 17:158(J)" as the charter transportation cross-reference reflects older subsection lettering — current 17:158(J) addresses loading/unloading procedures; the BESE charter transportation plan now references R.S. 17:158 generally. Negative search paths for the silence findings: (a) Max Contract Term and Rebid — searched legis.la.gov (full text of R.S. 17:158, 17:158.1-158.7, 17:491-500), the Louisiana Administrative Code Title 28 Part CXIII Bulletin 119 in full (February 2023 publication), LDOE School Transportation policy page, LDOE Office of School Choice FAQ Public Bid Law (rev. 07/2025), BESE policy pages, the Louisiana Legislative Auditor's Public Bid Law FAQ (Rev. 02/2026) in full (65-page document; Q.86-Q.90 cover services), LASBO Workshop 11 (Public Bid Law) materials, and the LDOE charter transportation plan. Searched terms included: "school bus contract term," "pupil transportation contract renewal," "transportation services contract competitive bidding," "RS 17:158 contract term," "RS 38:2211 transportation services," "Louisiana attorney general opinion school bus contract term." No statute, regulation, AG opinion, or LDOE/LASBO guidance was located that prescribes a term cap, a mandatory rebid trigger, or a renewal mechanism for private student-transportation services contracts.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: La. R.S. 17:158 — School buses for transportation of students; employment of bus operators; alternative means of transportation; improvement of school bus turnarounds; loading and unloading students. Subsection (A)(4): 'any city, parish, or other local public school board may employ school bus operators as defined in R.S. 17:491. However, nothing in this Section shall prohibit a city, parish, or other local public school board from entering into contracts or mutual agreements for providing school bus transportation.' Amendment history through Acts 2023, No. 362, §1.
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Reflects amendments through Acts 2023, No. 362, §1; Louisiana State Legislature official site. Subsections A-K verified present on re-verification 2026-06-23
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; Special Education (general authority)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Authorizes school boards to enter into contracts or mutual agreements for providing school bus transportation; silent on max term, rebid, and renewal mechanics. Subsection (H) requires State Board approval before discontinuation of transportation services.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: La. R.S. 17:491 — School bus operators; certification. Defines 'school bus operator' as 'any employee of any city, parish, or other local public school board whose duty it is to transport students in any board school bus.'
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Current text per Louisiana State Legislature; amendment history shown through 2017
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Defines school bus operator as a school-board employee, establishing the employee/owner-operator structure that frames Louisiana contracting

sourceLedgerRows.citation: La. R.S. 17:492 — Probation and tenure of bus operators (tenure abolished prospectively for operators starting employment on or after July 1, 2012, per Acts 2012, No. 704)
sourceLedgerRows.officialUrl: https://legis.la.gov/legis/LawSearch.aspx (Title 17 search); cross-referenced through Justia 2024 Louisiana Laws
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Current per Louisiana State Legislature (2024 Louisiana laws version)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Tenure-eligibility cutoff at July 1, 2012; operators starting after that date are removable per local personnel policy

sourceLedgerRows.citation: La. R.S. 17:493.1 — Filling route vacancies (offers vacant routes by tenure/seniority sequence; provides for buyer of retiring operator's bus at appraised value where retirement is for documented physical disability and bus is within 5 model years)
sourceLedgerRows.officialUrl: https://legis.la.gov/legis/LawSearch.aspx (Title 17 official search); cross-verified through 2024 Louisiana laws (Justia mirror used only as pathfinder for section ID)
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Current per Louisiana State Legislature (2024 Louisiana laws version)
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required (closest Louisiana analogue for individual-operator vacancies); Notes
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Tenure/seniority-based, non-competitive route-vacancy filling procedure; not a true competitive rebid. Tenured operator (greatest seniority) offered first by mail; if declined, in order of seniority; then probationary; then substitute. Owner-retirement carve-out for buyers of retiring operator's bus at appraised value.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: La. R.S. 17:496 — Minimum salaries (operators of buses fourteen feet or longer; less than fourteen feet and station-wagon special-education routes)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Sets minimum salaries for bus operators ($6,000 annually nine-month basis for buses 14 feet or longer; $5,000 for smaller vehicles/station wagons); underlies compensation structure for individual operators

sourceLedgerRows.citation: La. R.S. 17:497 — School bus operators; compensation (operational mileage rate; basis for frozen mileage)
sourceLedgerRows.officialUrl: https://legis.la.gov/Legis/Law.aspx (Title 17 search); cross-verified through 2025 Louisiana laws
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Current per Louisiana State Legislature (2025 Louisiana laws version)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Anchors the 7-year (new bus) / 5-year (used bus) frozen-mileage protection elaborated in Bulletin 119 §1703; sets minimum operational pay rates per mile

sourceLedgerRows.citation: La. R.S. 17:497.1 — Operation compensation for certain school bus operators. APPLIES IN ST. LANDRY PARISH ONLY. Statute states verbatim: 'the provisions of this section shall apply in St. Landry Parish only.' 5-year compensation protection where route altered by court order; operator must own the bus. Acts 1970, No. 333.
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: CORRECTED in Stage 2: This is a St. Landry Parish-only provision, not a statewide compensation protection (Stage 1 corrected). 5-year floor at three-year average compensation for owner-operators whose routes are altered by court order or court-mandated busing plan.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: La. R.S. 38:2211 et seq. — Public Bid Law (public works and materials/supplies). Most relevant: R.S. 38:2212 (advertisement and letting); R.S. 38:2212.1 (materials/supplies thresholds and exceptions).
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Current per Louisiana State Legislature; effective February 1, 2026 thresholds reflected in LLA FAQ (A.1)
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required (negative — confirms services contracts are outside the Public Bid Law)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: By its text and as construed by the Legislative Auditor, the Public Bid Law does not reach services contracts (only public works and materials/supplies); transportation services therefore not subject to mandatory competitive bidding

sourceLedgerRows.citation: LAC 28:CXIII (Bulletin 119 — Louisiana School Transportation Specifications and Procedures), promulgated by BESE pursuant to R.S. 17:158, 17:160-161, 17:164-166. Key sections re-verified: §307 (Retaining School Bus Drivers); §309 (Tenure and Termination of Bus Drivers); §1505 (Routes: Filling Vacancies — cross-references R.S. 17:493.1); §1703 (Salary Compensation: Frozen Mileage); §2513(D) (Insurance for Contracted Services).

State law authorizes LEAs contracting for the use of privately owned school buses to procure contracts on a fleet or group basis for the owners who are insuring the vehicles.' sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Treats only public-works contracts as requiring competitive bidding for school boards; the FAQ contains a Public Works table (bid required if contract exceeds $150,000, then publishing thresholds, etc.) and does not impose any rebid duty on transportation services contracts.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Multiple parish school district policies — district materials used only to assess variance, not to establish statewide law (West Baton Rouge ED-23; Bossier ED-23; Zachary ED-23; Orleans/NOLA Public Schools 17:0158-6).
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: District Variance (no uniform contract-term or rebid provision found across surveyed districts)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: District policies recite R.S. 17:158 and adopt the Bulletin 119 personal-operation rule but do not impose a uniform contract-term or rebid requirement on private transportation contractors. Industry trade press (School Bus Fleet, First Student newsroom) reports a 5-year contract renewal in Jefferson Parish in 2016 — supports variance, but is not authoritative for statewide law.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: 'No statutory maximum found.'
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Some Title 17 section or LAC 28:CXIII chapter caps the term of a private student transportation services contract (e.g., 'not to exceed,' 'multi-year limit,' 'option to renew,' or 'successive' language).
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-fetched R.S. 17:158 in full via official legis.la.gov page and confirmed subsections A through K with no term cap. Searched the LAC 28:CXIII full text (Bulletin 119, February 2023 publication via mirror after doa.la.gov 404 on primary URL) for 'term,' 'years,' 'duration,' 'not exceed,' 'multi-year,' 'extension,' 'option,' 'successive' against transportation contract provisions. Cross-checked R.S. 17:158.1-158.7 and R.S. 17:491-500. Searched LLA Public Bid Law FAQ (Rev. 02/2026) for any duration cap on services contracts (none — services are outside the Public Bid Law entirely).
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. No primary or regulatory source caps the term of a private transportation services contract. R.S. 17:158 current form (subsections A-K) verified on official source.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Rebid Required: 'Not required by state law.'
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Some general procurement statute (R.S. 38:2211 et seq. Public Bid Law; R.S. 39:1551 et seq. Louisiana Procurement Code; or a local-government contracting statute) reaches school district transportation services contracts and mandates competitive selection at term-end.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-fetched and locally extracted LLA Public Bid Law FAQ (Revised 02/2026) — A.87 and A.88 confirm services contracts (including transportation) are outside the Public Bid Law. LDOE Office of School Choice FAQ (rev. 07/2025) confirms only public-works contracts require bidding. LASBO Workshop 11 verbatim text 'Public Bid Law does not apply to the purchase of Services — Professional or Otherwise' confirmed. Searched for any school-specific bid statute or AG opinion to the contrary — none found.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. Three converging official/authoritative sources affirm services contracts are outside mandatory bidding for local school boards.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics: 'No statewide renewal mechanism is prescribed' (with frozen-mileage and St. Landry court-order protections noted).
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: (a) The frozen-mileage protections in R.S. 17:497 / LAC 28:CXIII §1703 actually function as renewal-procedure caps for private services contracts; (b) R.S. 17:497.1 applies statewide (as Stage 1 implied), creating a 5-year compensation floor for any operator after court-ordered route change.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-fetched LAC 28:CXIII §1703(B) — verified verbatim: frozen mileage is a compensation-protection (not a contract-term) mechanism, applies only to individual contracted owner-operators, not fleet contracts. Re-fetched R.S. 17:497.1 on the official legis.la.gov page — confirmed verbatim: 'the provisions of this section shall apply in St. Landry Parish only.' Cross-verified via WebSearch and 2017 Louisiana laws.
challengeMatrixRows.result: REFUTED IN PART — St. Landry Parish-only scope of R.S. 17:497.1 corrected in the final Renewal Mechanics field value (Stage 1 had implied statewide applicability). Frozen mileage finding confirmed as compensation-protection floor (not a contract-term renewal).
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Adopted corrected scope; finding otherwise confirmed — High

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Statutory Citation: R.S. 17:158(A)(4) as primary authority; LAC 28:CXIII as primary regulatory authority.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: The cited URL https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=79904 does not return R.S. 17:158, or the quoted (A)(4) text does not appear, or the cited Bulletin 119 URL https://www.doa.la.gov/media/b0ehowxe/28v113.pdf does not produce the regulation.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=79904 — section title and (A)(4) quote verified verbatim, amendment history through Acts 2023, No. 362, §1 confirmed. Re-opened https://www.doa.la.gov/media/b0ehowxe/28v113.pdf — returned HTTP 404 on re-verification (was reachable for Stage 1). Verified Bulletin 119 text against EBR Schools mirror (https://ebrschools.org/CAPS/Bulletins/Bulletin119.pdf) and Cornell LII publication (https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/louisiana/title-28/part-CXIII).
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed for primary statutory citation; partially verified for Bulletin 119 (primary doa.la.gov URL unreachable but content verified via mirrors). Citation retained because the doa.la.gov URL is the official publisher and the substantive text is unchanged.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Confirmed — High (with note on doa.la.gov URL 404)

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Either (a) state-level guidance enforces uniform contract-term/rebid practices, supporting a 'No' label; or (b) clear evidence in two or more district policy/procurement manuals documents a uniform variance, supporting a 'Yes' label.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Searched LDOE bulletins, BESE policy, LLA advisories, AG opinions, LASBO publications, and 8+ parish policy manuals. Found no state-level uniform guidance and no authoritative cross-parish synthesis. Industry press (School Bus Fleet, First Student newsroom) confirms Jefferson Parish 5-year First Student contract renewal in 2016 (https://www.schoolbusfleet.com/news/louisiana-district-extends-contract-with-first-student) — supports the existence of variance but is not a primary source.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Unresolved — Unknown stands. No primary/authoritative source documents either uniformity or variance at the state level; trade press confirms multi-year private contracts are used in at least one parish, but that is district practice rather than authoritative state-level evidence.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Special Education: 'Different services obligations, same contract framework' (no separate procurement statute/term-cap/rebid/renewal regime).
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A separate Louisiana provision addresses contracts for IEP/special-education transportation, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or alternative student transportation with different term/rebid/renewal rules.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Reviewed LAC 28:CXIII Chapter 21 (Transporting Students with Disabilities; §2101-2107) — service-quality regime, no contract-term/rebid rule. Reviewed Chapter 19 §1905 (Foster Care) — service obligations, no contracting overlay. Searched for IDEA-specific state procurement carve-outs — none found. Federal duties (IDEA, Section 504, McKinney-Vento, ESSA Title IX) govern service obligations, not state contract/procurement.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. No separate special-education procurement regime; general R.S. 17:158(A)(4) authorization applies.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Currentness — was R.S. 17:158 amended after Acts 2023, No. 362?
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 2024 or 2025 session amendment to R.S. 17:158 introduced a term cap, rebid trigger, or renewal mechanism for private school bus transportation contracts.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Searched legis.la.gov bill lookups for 2024-2025 amendments. Identified 2024 SB 416 (SLS 24RS-564) which proposed to amend (K) and enact (L) — current statute as published on legis.la.gov still ends at (K), indicating SB 416 did not enact the (L) language (or was modified). No 2024 or 2025 amendment to R.S. 17:158, 17:491-497, or 17:493.1 introducing a contract term cap, rebid requirement, or renewal mechanism was identified.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. No post-2023 amendment changes the substantive findings on Max Term, Rebid, or Renewal.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Applicability boundary — does the Louisiana Procurement Code (R.S. 39:1551 et seq.) reach local school boards?
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: The LPC binds local school boards generally, not just executive-branch state agencies, and could impose competitive procurement on transportation services.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: LLA Public Bid Law FAQ A.91 and A.92 confirmed: LPC is for executive-branch state agencies; local entities may CHOOSE by ordinance/resolution to adopt LPC for materials/supplies (R.S. 38:2212.1 cross-reference). No mandatory LPC application to local school boards for services contracts.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. LPC does not bind local school boards for transportation services unless affirmatively adopted (and even then, for materials/supplies).

perFieldVerdicts.note: Re-verified R.S. 17:158 on official legis.la.gov page; subsections A-K contain no term cap. No transportation-specific or general procurement statute caps the term of a private student-transportation services contract.

Re-verified LLA Public Bid Law FAQ A.87 and A.88 verbatim from official PDF (Revised 02/2026). Services contracts (including transportation) are outside the Public Bid Law for local political subdivisions; no mandatory rebid duty at term-end.

Stage 1 implied R.S. 17:497.1 was a statewide compensation protection. The official statute (re-verified at https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=81120) states 'the provisions of this section shall apply in St. Landry Parish only.' CORRECTED FINDING ADOPTED: R.S. 17:497.1 applies in St. Landry Parish only. The broader finding — no statewide renewal mechanism prescribed — is confirmed. Frozen mileage (R.S. 17:497 / LAC 28:CXIII §1703(B)) verified verbatim.

Primary citation R.S. 17:158(A)(4) re-verified verbatim on official legis.la.gov page. Bulletin 119 primary URL (doa.la.gov) returned 404 on re-verification; substantive text verified via EBR Schools mirror and Cornell LII publication. Citation retained as official publisher reference.

Unknown label confirmed as appropriate. Industry trade press documents at least one multi-year RFP-procured contract (Jefferson Parish/First Student, 2016) but no primary/authoritative state-level evidence establishes either uniform application or systematic variance.
Re-verified LAC 28:CXIII Chapters 19 and 21; no separate special-education procurement regime. General R.S. 17:158(A)(4) applies; federal law governs service obligations.

Substantive Bulletin 119 text verified via EBR Schools mirror and Cornell LII; citation retained as the official publisher reference.)

sourceValidationStatus: partially verified — see note: All Stage 1 primary statutory citations were re-opened on legis.la.gov and confirmed verbatim. The load-bearing primary citation behind Max-Term/Rebid/Renewal (R.S. 17:158) was fully re-verified. The Bulletin 119 official PDF (https://www.doa.la.gov/media/b0ehowxe/28v113.pdf) returned HTTP 404 on re-verification 2026-06-23 — substantive text verified via EBR Schools mirror and Cornell LII publication; finding stands. R.S. 17:497.1 was REFUTED-AND-CORRECTED for geographic scope (Stage 1 implied statewide; corrected to St. Landry Parish only).
adversarialVerdictSummary: confirmed (5/6); Renewal Mechanics refuted-and-corrected on St. Landry Parish scope of R.S. 17:497.1 (broader finding stands); District Variance unresolved (Unknown label confirmed as appropriate).
verificationNotes:
Stage 2 (Adversarial Validation) re-verification, 2026-06-23, Louisiana lane.

SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT (load-bearing primary citations):
(1) R.S. 17:158 — re-opened at https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=79904 via WebFetch. Confirmed section title 'School buses for transportation of students; employment of bus operators…' Confirmed subsection (A)(4) verbatim: 'For the purposes of this Subsection, any city, parish, or other local public school board may employ school bus operators as defined in R.S. 17:491. However, nothing in this Section shall prohibit a city, parish, or other local public school board from entering into contracts or mutual agreements for providing school bus transportation.' Confirmed amendment history through Acts 2023, No. 362, §1. Subsections present: A through K (no L or M). No subsection caps term, mandates bidding, or sets renewal mechanics for private transportation contracts.
(2) R.S. 17:497.1 — re-opened at https://legis.la.gov/legis/Law.aspx?d=81120 via WebFetch and cross-verified via WebSearch (Justia 2017 Louisiana laws). The statute expressly states: 'provided however that the provisions of this section shall apply in St. Landry Parish only.' THIS REFUTES STAGE 1's implication of statewide applicability. Stage 2 correction adopted in the Renewal Mechanics and Notes fields. Single bounded re-research pass triggered; resolved.
(3) LAC 28:CXIII Bulletin 119 — primary doa.la.gov URL returned HTTP 404. Re-verified Bulletin 119 substantive text via mirror at https://ebrschools.org/CAPS/Bulletins/Bulletin119.pdf (East Baton Rouge Parish CAPS) — saved locally and text-extracted with pdftotext. Confirmed verbatim: §307 (Retaining School Bus Drivers); §309(B) (operators starting July 1, 2012 or later not eligible for tenure); §1505 (Routes Filling Vacancies, cross-references R.S. 17:493.1); §1703(B) verbatim ('Frozen mileage guarantees that the contract owner/operator cannot be penalized by a reduction of compensated mileage … for a period of seven years when a new bus is purchased or five years when a used bus not more than five model year old is purchased'); §2513(D) verbatim ('Insurance for Contracted Services. State law authorizes LEAs contracting for the use of privately owned school buses to procure contracts on a fleet or group basis for the owners who are insuring the vehicles'). Publication date: 'Louisiana Administrative Code February 2023' confirmed in footer.
(4) Louisiana Legislative Auditor Public Bid Law FAQ — re-opened the official PDF at https://app.lla.state.la.us/llala.nsf/87BD5C74CB17E03686257AB8006F37DE/$FILE/Public%20Bid%20Law%20FAQ.pdf via WebFetch (returned PDF saved locally) and extracted with pdftotext. Confirmed 'REVISED 02/2026' on every footer (65-page document). Confirmed Q.87 and A.87 verbatim: A.87 'No. Local governments and political subdivisions are not required to advertise, receive bids or engage in competitive negotiations for contracts for professional services.' Confirmed Q.88 and A.88 verbatim: A.88 'No. The Public Bid Law is relevant to the procurement of materials and supplies and public works, not services.' Confirmed A.1 thresholds effective February 1, 2026 ($60,000 materials/supplies; $260,000 public works).
(5) LDOE Office of School Choice FAQ — re-opened at https://doe.louisiana.gov/docs/default-source/school-choice/faq-public-bid-law.pdf via WebFetch (PDF saved locally and extracted). Confirmed footer date 07/2025. Confirmed scope is public-works only for school boards; no transportation services contracting rule.
(6) LASBO Workshop 11 — re-opened at https://cdn.ymaws.com/lasbo.org/resource/collection/E9C17B05-DDC1-4796-A03E-2665F6A408BE/Workshop_11_Public_Bid_Law.pdf via WebFetch (PDF saved locally and extracted). Confirmed verbatim slide text: 'Public Bid Law does not apply to the purchase of Services — Professional or Otherwise.'

CURRENT-LAW CHECK: R.S. 17:158 amendment history through Acts 2023, No. 362, §1. 2024 SB 416 (SLS 24RS-564) identified but did not enact substantive contract-term/rebid/renewal language (current statute ends at subsection K). No 2024 or 2025 session amendment to R.S. 17:158, 17:491-497, or 17:493.1 introducing a contract term cap, rebid requirement, or renewal mechanism was identified. Bulletin 119 historical notes show amendments through LR 49:244 (February 2023) — no pending rulemaking on contract terms identified.

ORCHESTRATION PROVENANCE (this run): Stage 2 re-verification completed via WebFetch and WebSearch (primary tools — firecrawl token expired mid-stage but was not required; equivalent verifications completed via WebFetch with local PDF text extraction). All primary statutory citations re-opened on legis.la.gov. The one Stage 1 finding that was REFUTED-AND-CORRECTED concerns the geographic scope of R.S. 17:497.1 (Stage 1 implied statewide; corrected to St. Landry Parish only). No silent fallback or refusal event triggered.
